Quick issues

⚠ Bleed margin 1mm
Required: 3mm. Risk of edge cropping during trim.

"Grand Opening" text 6mm from edge (safe: 12mm).

File properties

Filenamedesign-final.pdf
Size8.4 MB
Pages1
Color modeCMYK
Resolution300 DPI
Color profileSWOP v2
Fonts4 embedded

Customer message

"Please match our brand colors exactly — navy #2B3A8A and gold #FFE35A. Edges should be clean (we'll be displaying at the entrance). Need by Friday for our Saturday opening."

Pre-press check report

5 passed · 2 warnings · 0 failed
Resolution OK
300 DPI at print size (16500×20400 px) — exceeds 300 DPI minimum for outdoor banners.
Color mode CMYK
CMYK color mode with SWOP v2 profile. Matches print requirements for 15oz vinyl.
Fonts embedded
4 fonts properly embedded: Impact, Helvetica Neue Bold, Arial, Georgia. No missing fonts.
Bleed margin too small
Current bleed: 1mm. Required: 3mm minimum for HD vinyl banner.
Impact: Risk of white edge showing after trim, or critical content cropped.
→ Auto-fix with AI: extend bleed to 3mm
Text near edge
"Grand Opening" text sits 6mm from edge. Recommended safe zone: 12mm.
Impact: Text may appear cut off when viewed at distance.
→ Highlight on canvas
No transparency issues
All transparency flattened. No risk of unexpected rendering on press.
Image quality OK
No low-resolution raster images detected. All photos at 300+ DPI at scale.

Comments & feedback

Visible to admin + customer
MK
Mike R. (production) 1 hour ago
Bleed margin only 1mm. We can either:
1. Auto-extend by mirroring edges (safest, free)
2. Request new file from Marcus with 3mm bleed
3. Skip bleed but warn about possible edge cropping
HF
Hoyle (you) 45 mins ago
Let's auto-extend (option 1). Edges are solid color so mirroring will be clean. Need this by Friday — no time for back-and-forth.
MK
Mike R. (production) 30 mins ago
👍 Applying auto-fix now. Will save as v3 and queue for press. ETA: 2 days production + 1 day shipping.
HF

Event signage package

"We're hosting 25 events across Texas this fall. Each location needs 2 identical backdrops. Brand consistency is critical — all backdrops must match perfectly. We can pick up from Fort Pierce or pay for ground shipping. Net-30 payment preferred for our corporate account."

Hoyle (you) 1 hour ago
New prospect — events agency in Austin. If they accept this, they're likely B2B-A material. Pre-approve them for tier after first order. Price aggressively to win — competitor in Houston quoted $14K.

50 units is tight for 2 weeks but doable with rush slot. Confirm $800 rush fee works. If we lose rush, we miss Aug 15 event date.

/ Negotiate · counter-offer

Negotiate quote

Counter-offer mode

Customer wants to negotiate

Rachel Liu (Big Events Co.) sent a counter-offer on Q-2026-0028 · 1 hour ago. Review request below and respond.

Customer's counter-offer

Received 1 hour ago via customer portal
"Thanks for the quick turnaround. The pricing is close but we have a few asks:

1. Can you drop the rush production fee? We can flex the event date by 1 week if needed.
2. We're considering picking up from Fort Pierce — would save us $1,250 on freight. Can you confirm pickup is feasible for 50 units?

3. If we commit to this AND a follow-on Q4 order of similar size, can we get Net-30 instead of 50/50?

If we can land at ~$10,400 with Net-30, we'll sign today. — Rachel"
Our quote
$12,201
50/50 terms · margin 38%
Customer's ask
~$10,400
Net-30 · gap −$1,801 (−14.8%)

Their 3 asks · response builder

Ask #1: Drop rush production fee ($800)
Ask #2: Pickup instead of freight (save $1,250)
Ask #3: Net-30 + commit to Q4 follow-on order

Revised quote v2 · preview

Auto-calculated from selections above
Item Original Revised Change
Step & Repeat × 50 $9,750.00 $9,750.00 —
Setup & QC fee $450.00 $450.00 —
Rush production fee REMOVED $800.00 $0.00 −$800.00
Freight → Pickup CHANGED $1,250.00 $0.00 −$1,250.00
Original quote$12,201.43
Total reduction−$2,050.00 (−16.8%)
Revised total$10,151.43
vs customer's ask ($10,400)$249 under their target ✓
Payment terms50/50 (counter to their Net-30 ask)
Margin impact38% → 26% (still profitable)

Response message to customer

Send revised quote

Customer will receive email + portal notification with revised quote v2.

Margin warning

Margin drops from 38% → 26% after concessions. Still profitable but below 30% threshold.

💡 If they push further on price, do not go below $9,200 (20% margin floor).

Simulate next workflow step

Demo · choose customer action

Below are the 3 possible customer responses. Click one to jump to that workflow stage.

Customer accepts
Customer signs the quote and pays deposit. Quote converts to order.
Customer negotiates
Customer sends counter-offer or asks for changes. Build revised quote v2.
Customer declines
Customer declines with reason. Quote closes. Lead can be re-engaged later.

⚡ Preflight Quality Checker ENABLED

7-point quality scan: resolution, color mode, bleed, fonts, transparency, text proximity, integrity. Runs automatically on file upload.
When enabled, customers see a small "Skip AI check" option with a warning.
$0.01 per check Included in PRO plan up to 500/mo

🔍 AI Image Upscale ENABLED

AI-powered image upscaling 2x to 8x. Use to bring low-resolution images up to print quality (300 DPI).

Auto-suggest Upscale when DPI < (target: 300 DPI for print)

FREE (within fair-use 100/mo)

Preflight check thresholds

Check Pass threshold Warning threshold Failure threshold Severity
Resolution / DPI
Ensures sharp print quality
 Critical
Color mode
CMYK for print accuracy
 Medium
Bleed margin
Edge bleed for trimming
 Critical
Font embedding
All fonts embedded in file
 Critical
Transparency
Flattened for print compatibility
 Medium
Text proximity
Distance from edge / safe zone
 Low
File integrity
No corruption, valid format
 Critical

Per-tier overrides for this product

Optional · overrides global tier discounts
Tier Global discount This product override Effective price (4×8)
B2C — — $48.00

B2B · B 15% 12% (volume cap) $42.24

💡 Why override? When global tier discounts would push price below our cost+30% margin floor, we cap the discount. B2B · B and B2B · C have product-specific caps for this banner.

Printing FAQ · 8 questions

PRINTING What resolution should my design be?
We recommend 300 DPI at the final print size for sharp results. For an HD Vinyl Banner, that means your file should be at least 21,600 × 10,800 pixels for a 72×36in banner.
Viewed 847× · Helpfulness 4.7/5
FILE FORMAT What's the difference between RGB and CMYK?
RGB is for screens · CMYK is for print. Our AI will auto-convert RGB to CMYK using SWOP v2 profile, but starting in CMYK gives you predictable color.
Viewed 523× · Helpfulness 4.5/5
BLEED What is bleed and why do I need it?
Bleed is extra design area (3mm) that extends beyond the trim line. It prevents thin white edges if cutting is slightly off. Our Online Designer adds bleed automatically.
Viewed 412× · Helpfulness 4.8/5
MATERIAL Indoor vs outdoor vinyl — which to pick?
For events <30 days outdoor, our 13oz vinyl works. For permanent outdoor use (storefronts), choose 15oz with UV inks — rated 3+ years.
Viewed 287× · Helpfulness 4.6/5

About / Use case

Full-service marketing agency specializing in real estate, retail, and event promotions for Austin-area businesses. Primary signage needs: HD banners for grand openings, real estate riders for property listings, and event step & repeat backdrops. Repeat customer with consistent quarterly campaigns. Excellent print-ready file quality.

Email template

Customize the invitation email template used for all sends.

Tips

💡 Higher acceptance: Pre-assign a tier so the business sees concrete value upfront.

📧 Personal touch: Use the contact's name in the message.

⏰ Timing: Tuesday–Thursday 10–11am ET has highest open rates.

Page 1 of 5

Pricing rules

Add rule

Define tier discounts, bulk pricing, and per-product overrides. Rules cascade — higher priority wins.

Tier discount ladder

Apply percentage off retail prices based on customer tier
Tier Discount % Minimum order Payment terms Free shipping Customers
B2C — Guest/Member — None Credit card (pre-pay) Over $200 719 Edit
B2B — Tier A 10% $100 Credit card or invoice Over $150 52 Edit
B2B — Tier B 15% $250 Net-15 Over $100 48 Edit
B2B — Tier C 20% $500 Net-30 Always free 24 Edit
★ VIP 24% None Net-30 + custom Always free + rush 4 Edit

Quantity-based discounts

Stack on top of tier discount — applies to all products by default
Quantity Extra discount Applies to Status
5+ units +3% All products Active Edit
10+ units +7% All products Active Edit
25+ units +12% All products Active Edit
50+ units +18% Banners, Yard signs Active Edit
100+ units Custom quote All products Active Edit

Special rules

Product-specific overrides, promo codes, and seasonal pricing
Rule name Trigger Effect Priority Status
Grand Opening promo Code: OPENING20 20% off HD Banners 10 Active Edit
Rush order surcharge Production speed: 24h +$15 flat fee 5 Active Edit
Real estate rider rebate Tier A+ · 6+ riders $2 off per rider 3 Active Edit
Free pre-press review Any B2B order Skip $5 review fee 2 Active Edit
Holiday sale 2025 Date range expired 15% off everything 1 Expired Re-enable
1–10 of 18 pricing

Brand Templates

New brand template

Centralized template library that you assign to one or many Corporate clients. Customers see only the templates assigned to their company under their Corporate → Brand Templates page. Lock specs to enforce brand consistency, or leave templates editable for in-house designers.

Active templates
12
+3 this month
Corporates served
8 / 128
Of B2B store base
Uses YTD
847
+24% MoM
Drafts
3
Awaiting assignment
⚡
Brand Templates vs. Designer templates
Designer templates are the store-wide catalog shown to all customers in the Online Designer. Brand Templates are private to a specific Corporate client — locked specs, locked brand colors, ready for their team to order. Use these for B2B clients with strict brand guidelines (Chen Marketing, Riverside Realty, etc.).

Rule definition

vs $100 tier baseline
Will auto-extend if not unchecked

⚙ Printing Options binding · synced

↻ Last synced 5 min ago

This pricing rule references the global Printing Options table. When admin changes paper, finishing, or quantity tiers in Printing Options, prices here re-calculate automatically. View Printing Options →

⚡ Bulk assign templates

Onboard a new Corporate client by assigning multiple templates at once. Useful when a new B2B store is set up and needs a starter library.

1 · Select target corporates
2 · Select templates to assign
Summary: Will create 2 assignments (1 corporate × 2 templates) with default 🔒 Locked setting. Emails will be sent to 1 admin.

＋ New Brand Template

Set up a new corporate-shared template. After creating, you'll be able to design it in Designer Canvas and assign it to one or many Corporate clients.

💲 Edit per-corporate pricing rule

Override the tier default with a custom rule for this specific corporate. Synced with Printing Options when applicable.

Tier B baseline: 15% · This is +3.5% bonus

📨 Invite a business to your B2B store

Send a co-branded invitation for a business to join your B2B program. They'll fill in their company profile and you'll review the application.

💲 Add new pricing rule

Choose what kind of rule to create. Each rule binds to specific Printing Options and applies on top of tier discounts.

🎨 Add Designer template

Designer templates are the store-wide catalog visible to all customers in the Online Designer's Templates panel. For corporate-private templates, use Brand Templates instead.
